message:
feat(core): COLLATE NOCASE in IN/BETWEEN/MIN/MAX + COUNT-IN fast path

Two related additions to the connection-level expression evaluator
plus their conformance/regression tests.

1. Collation-aware comparisons (closes the s69cj MIN/MAX gap)
   Previously, IN/BETWEEN/MIN/MAX for TEXT COLLATE NOCASE columns fell
   through to cmp_values() which does binary comparison — so
   'Alice' COLLATE NOCASE IN ('alice', 'BOB') returned false instead
   of true, and MIN/MAX returned the binary-min instead of the
   nocase-min.

   New resolve_operand_collation() walks Expr::Collate / Expr::Column
   against the live schema list and returns the effective collation.
   On a non-NULL collation:
   - BETWEEN uses cmp_values_collated_snapshot()
   - IN list/subquery/table paths do the same, with a per-probe
     snapshot of the collation registry held outside the row loop
   - MIN/MAX aggregates call compute_aggregate_ext_collated()

   The cached-HashSet optimization for large IN literal lists (wwqen.2
   B2.3) now skips when a non-BINARY collation is active, because
   HashSet uses binary equality and would miss case-insensitive hits.

   s55: reactivated test_conformance_collate_nocase_ops_s69cj (minus
   the MIN/MAX query which still needs VDBE aggregate collation
   plumbing) and added a dedicated test_conformance_collate_nocase_
   min_max_s69cj2 guarded with #[ignore] documenting that gap.

2. Direct MemDB fast path for COUNT(*) WHERE col IN (literal_list)
   (bd-1dp9.6.2)
   After IN-subquery rewriting the subquery becomes a literal list. A
   new try_direct_count_star_in_list() detects the exact shape:
     SELECT COUNT(*) FROM <single table> WHERE <col> IN (<all literals>)
   with no DISTINCT / GROUP BY / HAVING / ORDER BY / LIMIT / joins
   / CTE / window / compound. When it matches, we build a HashSet of
   the literal values and count MemDB rows with a direct iter, fully
   bypassing VDBE codegen and interpretation. Rowid-alias columns are
   resolved through rowid_alias_columns so INTEGER PRIMARY KEY
   lookups still compare against the physical rowid.

   Two regression tests in in_exists_regression.rs:
   - test_count_star_in_list_fast_path_correctness: 1000 products ×
     10 categories, spot-checks three subquery shapes.
   - guard_count_star_in_subquery_completes_within_budget: 10K rows,
     requires the fast path to finish inside 500ms (pre-B2 IN
     catastrophe took >3s at this size).

Both changes keep the VDBE path as the fallback — we only take the
direct path when every structural guard matches.
